nodes.title: Data Cleaning and Preprocessing Techniques
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Address common data quality issues: handling missing values, dealing with outliers, data type conversion, encoding categorical variables (one-hot, label encoding), and feature scaling.

nodes.title: Data Storytelling and Communication
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Teach how to effectively communicate data insights. Focus on structuring a data story, choosing appropriate visualizations, and presenting findings clearly to non-technical audiences.

nodes.title: Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Teach how to summarize data using descriptive statistics and visualize data using Matplotlib and Seaborn. Cover common plots (histograms, scatter plots, box plots, bar charts) to uncover patterns and insights.

nodes.title: Understanding Data: Types and Sources
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Explain different types of data (numerical, categorical, ordinal, nominal), data sources (databases, APIs, files), and basic data terminology.

nodes.title: Introduction to Data Science
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Define data science, its importance, common applications, and the typical roles within the field. Discuss the data science lifecycle.

nodes.title: Introduction to Machine Learning
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Explain what machine learning is, its types (supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement), common use cases, and the basic ML workflow (data prep, model training, evaluation).

nodes.title: Advanced Model Evaluation and Selection
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Discuss concepts like overfitting, underfitting, bias-variance trade-off. Introduce cross-validation, basic hyperparameter tuning (Grid Search), and feature importance.

nodes.title: Next Steps in Your Data Science Journey
nodes.displayTitle: Next Steps in Your Data Science Journey
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Provide guidance on further learning paths (deep learning, big data, specialized domains), ethical considerations in data science, and advice on building a portfolio.

nodes.title: Supervised Learning: Classification Models
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Introduce Logistic Regression and Decision Trees for classification. Cover concepts, scikit-learn implementation, and evaluation metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrix).

nodes.title: Supervised Learning: Regression Models
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Focus on Linear Regression. Explain the concept, implementation using scikit-learn, and evaluation metrics (MAE, MSE, R-squared). Discuss assumptions and limitations.

nodes.title: Unsupervised Learning: Clustering
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Explain the concept of unsupervised learning and introduce K-Means clustering. Cover the algorithm, how to choose K, and basic interpretation of clusters.

nodes.title: Introduction to Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Provide a gentle introduction to OOP concepts: classes, objects, attributes, and methods. Demonstrate creating a simple class with an `__init__` method.

nodes.title: Project: Command-Line To-Do List Application
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Guide students through building a simple command-line to-do list application that can add, view, mark as complete, and save/load tasks from a file. This project should integrate many previously learned concepts.

nodes.title: Conditional Statements (if, elif, else)
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Introduce `if`, `elif`, and `else` statements. Cover nested conditionals and practical examples like decision-making programs.

nodes.title: Data Structure: Dictionaries
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Introduce dictionaries: creation, accessing values by keys, adding/modifying entries, and common dictionary methods. Explain key-value pairs.

nodes.title: Error Handling (try-except)
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Introduce `try`, `except`, `else`, and `finally` blocks. Explain common types of errors and how to handle them gracefully.

nodes.title: File Input/Output
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Cover opening, reading from, and writing to text files. Introduce `with open()` statement for safe file handling. Discuss different modes ('r', 'w', 'a').

nodes.title: Functions: Organizing Your Code
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Cover defining functions, parameters, arguments, return values, default arguments, keyword arguments, and basic scope (local vs. global).

nodes.title: User Input and Output
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Focus on the `print()` function (formatting output) and the `input()` function for getting user input. Emphasize type conversion for input.

nodes.title: Introduction to Python and Setup
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Explain what Python is, its uses, and guide users through installing Python and a suitable IDE (like VS Code) or using an online interpreter. Cover 'Hello, World!'.

nodes.title: Data Structure: Lists
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Introduce lists: creation, accessing elements (indexing, slicing), modifying, adding/removing elements, and common list methods. Emphasize mutability.

nodes.title: Loops (for and while)
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Explain `for` loops (with `range()`) and `while` loops. Cover `break` and `continue` statements. Provide examples for iteration.

nodes.title: Modules and Packages: Reusing Code
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Explain how to import and use modules. Introduce common built-in modules (e.g., `math`, `random`). Briefly touch upon creating simple custom modules.

nodes.title: Operators in Python
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Explain arithmetic, assignment, comparison, logical, identity, and membership operators with practical examples.

nodes.title: Basic Syntax, Variables, and Data Types
nodes.displayTitle: Basic Syntax, Variables, and Data Types
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Cover basic syntax rules, comments, declaring variables, and fundamental data types: integers, floats, strings, and booleans. Explain type() function.

nodes.title: Data Structure: Sets
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Explain sets: creation, adding/removing elements, and set operations (union, intersection, difference). Emphasize uniqueness and unordered nature.

nodes.title: Data Structure: Tuples
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Explain tuples as immutable sequences. Cover creation, accessing elements, and when to use tuples instead of lists.

nodes.title: Virtual Environments and Pip
nodes.subject: python
nodes.description: Explain the importance of virtual environments. Guide users through creating and activating a virtual environment. Introduce `pip` for installing external packages.
